schools.title: Schools of Music
schools.subtitle: Four Continents, One Tradition
schools.description: Follow the melody across India, Bangladesh, the United States, and Canada — four schools united by the living tradition of Rabindrasangeet and Bengali performing arts.

professor.title: Prof. (Dr.) Rezwana Choudhury Bannya
professor.description: Padma Shri laureate, Independence Day Award recipient, and one of the world's foremost voices in Rabindrasangeet — founder of Shurer Dhara and Music for Development.

mfd.title: Music for Development
mfd.subtitle: Changing Lives Through Music
mfd.description: Since 2009, Music for Development has used the transformative power of music to uplift underprivileged children in Bangladesh — beginning as a UNDP-funded project and growing into a movement.

hero.title: Shurer Dhara
hero.tagline: The living tradition of Tagore's music, carried across continents by Prof. (Dr.) Rezwana Choudhury Bannya

about.title: About Shurer Dhara
about.body: Founded in 1992 by Prof. (Dr.) Rezwana Choudhury Bannya at the instruction of her guru Kanika Bandopadhyay, Shurer Dhara is Bangladesh's foremost institution for Rabindrasangeet and Bengali performing arts. What began as a single classroom in Dhaka has grown into a global network with branches in India, the United States, and Canada — training thousands of students and keeping the gharana of Tagore's music alive across generations.
about.missionBody: To carry the living tradition of Rabindrasangeet across generations and continents — the specific style handed down from Shailajaranjan Majumdar through Kanika Bandopadhyay to Prof. Bannya, and now to students around the world.

beginner.title: Foundation
beginner.desc: Breathing, posture, basic sur and lay — the building blocks of vocal music.

intermediate.title: Exploration
intermediate.desc: Raga introduction, tala, and expressive techniques for developing singers.

advanced.title: Mastery
advanced.desc: Khayal, thumri, and performance preparation for accomplished vocalists.

schools.subtitle: Four Continents, One Tradition
schools.title: Schools of Music
schools.intro: What began as a single classroom in Dhaka has grown into a global network of four schools — each rooted in the same tradition, each adapting to its cultural landscape. Shurer Dhara carries the melody of Rabindrasangeet and Bengali performing arts to every corner of the world.

schools.description: The birthplace of the tradition. Shurer Dhara's Kolkata chapter is rooted in the spiritual heartland of Rabindrasangeet, offering rigorous training in classical Bengali vocal music and Tagore's compositions.
The main centre of Shurer Dhara, based in Dhaka. Founded by Prof. (Dr.) Rezwana Choudhury Bannya, this school is also home to Music for Development and serves hundreds of students each year.

Shurer Dhara's North American presence extends to the vibrant South Asian diaspora community in New York. Monthly workshops, performance events, and regular classes bridge East and West through music.
Canada's growing Bengali community found a musical home in Toronto. Shurer Dhara Canada runs classes for all ages and regularly participates in multicultural festivals across Ontario.

professor.name: Prof. (Dr.) Rezwana Choudhury Bannya
professor.tagline: Vocalist. Educator. Cultural Ambassador. Padma Shri Laureate. For over four decades, Prof. (Dr.) Rezwana Choudhury Bannya has carried the living tradition of Rabindrasangeet across continents and generations.
professor.quote: Music means happiness and love. With love and your support we can change the lives of underprivileged children of Bangladesh.
professor.bio1: Born in Rangpur to a family steeped in Rabindrik culture — her father Mazher Khan a civil servant, her mother Esmat Khan a teacher, both devoted admirers of Tagore — Rezwana grew up as 'Bannya', a nickname drawn from the heroine of Tagore's beloved novel Shesher Kabita. She trained at Chhayanat, Dhaka, before winning an ICCR scholarship that took her to Visva-Bharati University, Santiniketan, in 1975. There she studied under the greatest living masters of Rabindrasangeet — Shailajaranjan Majumdar, Santidev Ghosh — and most intimately under Kanika Bandopadhyay (Mohordi), who became her mentor, guru, and lifelong confidante.
professor.bio2: In 1992, returning to Dhaka with the confidence of a fully realised artist, and at Mohordi's direct instruction, Bannya founded Shurer Dhara. What began as a single classroom grew into Bangladesh's foremost institution for Rabindrasangeet and Bengali performing arts — with branches in India, the United States, and Canada, training thousands of students across generations. She is today Professor and Chairperson of the Department of Dance at Dhaka University, and an external PhD examiner at Rabindra Bharati University, India.
professor.bio3: Over four decades she has recorded more than 900 songs released across Germany, the United States, India, and Bangladesh — including over 100 albums, and was HMV's best-selling artist from 1999 to 2005. She has performed before heads of state, at UNESCO in Paris, at Symphony Space on Broadway in New York, and across Asia, Europe, and the Americas as a National Artist of Bangladesh. In 2009 she launched Music for Development, bringing music to underprivileged children in Dhaka's slum communities. In 2016 she received Bangladesh's highest civilian honour — the Independence Day Award (Sadhinota Podok). In 2024, the Government of India awarded her the Padma Shri — one of India's highest civilian honours.
professor.shrutiBody: On Tagore's 150th birth anniversary, Shurer Dhara launched Shrutigitabitan — a complete musical recording of all 2,233 songs in Tagore's Gitabitan. Performed by nearly 400 Bangladeshi-born singers, the album was launched on 29th December 2011 by Nobel Laureate Amartya Sen and Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina — a historic moment for Bengali music.

awards.title: Padma Shri
awards.body: Conferred by the Government of India — one of India's highest civilian honours — for lifetime dedication to Rabindrasangeet.

awards.title: PhD, Dhaka University
awards.body: Completed doctoral research on Rabindra Sangeet at the University of Dhaka.

awards.title: Honorary Doctorate of Arts
awards.body: Asian University for Women. Received from Chancellor Mrs. Cherie Blair and Vice Chancellor Prof. Nirmala Rao, OBE.

awards.title: Bangabhusan & Sangeet Mahasanman
awards.body: West Bengal's highest civilian honour and Sangeet Mahasanman — both awarded by the Government of West Bengal.

awards.title: Firoza Begum Golden Award
awards.body: Awarded in Bangladesh in honour of the legendary Nazrul singer Firoza Begum.

awards.title: Independence Day Award (Sadhinota Podok)
awards.body: Bangladesh's highest civilian honour, awarded by the President in recognition of lifelong contribution to music and culture.

awards.title: Sri Chinmoy Culture-Light Award
awards.body: Presented in the United States for her contribution to the promotion of Bengali culture internationally.

awards.title: Life Time Achievement Award (BTV & TRAB)
awards.body: Dual lifetime achievement recognition from Bangladesh Television (BTV) and the Television Reporters Association of Bangladesh.

awards.title: Rabindra Puroskar
awards.body: Bangla Academy, Bangladesh — for outstanding contribution to Rabindrasangeet.

awards.title: Sangeet Sanman
awards.body: Ministry of Culture, Government of West Bengal — for exceptional contribution to Indian classical music.

awards.title: Ananda Sangeet Award
awards.body: Ananda Bazar Group, India — twice awarded for the albums "Mor Dorodiya" and "Matir Dak".

awards.title: Bangladesh Film Journalists Association Award
awards.body: Recognised for her contributions to playback singing in Bengali cinema.

awards.title: Jaye Jaye Din Award
awards.body: Bangladesh — one of her earliest national recognitions.

professor.discographyBody: Recorded across Germany, the United States, India, and Bangladesh — including over 100 albums with HMV/Saregama, Bengal Foundation, Times of India, Alap, Sagarika, and more. HMV's best-selling artist from 1999 to 2005.
professor.globalBody: As a National Artist of Bangladesh, Bannya has performed across four continents — at UNESCO in Paris, the United Nations in New York, Symphony Space on Broadway, Queen Elizabeth Hall in London, and before heads of state in Delhi, Moscow, Tokyo, and beyond.
professor.ctaSubtitle: Learn from the tradition Prof. Bannya has dedicated her life to preserving.

achievements.title: Founded Shurer Dhara
achievements.desc: At the instruction of her guru Kanika Bandopadhyay, established Shurer Dhara in Dhaka — Bangladesh's premier institution for Rabindrasangeet and Bengali performing arts.

achievements.title: Music for Development
achievements.desc: Launched MFD with UNDP funding — bringing music, academic tutoring, and vocational skills to underprivileged children in Dhaka's slum communities.

achievements.title: Shrutigitabitan
achievements.desc: Curated a complete musical recording of Tagore's Gitabitan featuring nearly 400 Bangladeshi singers, launched by Nobel Laureate Amartya Sen and Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina.

achievements.title: Independence Day Award
achievements.desc: Received Bangladesh's highest civilian honour — the Sadhinota Podok (Independence Day Award) — in recognition of her lifelong contribution to music and culture.

achievements.title: Bangabhusan Award
achievements.desc: Awarded the Bangabhusan — West Bengal's highest civilian honour — by the Government of West Bengal for her extraordinary contribution to Bengali music and culture.

achievements.title: Padma Shri
achievements.desc: Conferred the Padma Shri by the Government of India — one of India's highest civilian honours — in recognition of her lifelong dedication to Rabindrasangeet and Bengali cultural heritage.

mfd.title: Music for Development
mfd.tagline: Changing lives through music. Since 2009, MFD has worked at the intersection of art and social development — proving that a song can open doors that nothing else can.
mfd.quote: Music means happiness and love. With love and your support we can change the lives of underprivileged children of Bangladesh.
mfd.storyTitle: Born from a belief that music belongs to everyone
mfd.story1: Music for Development (MFD) started in 2009 as a project of Shurer Dhara, a premier college for Rabindrasangeet and Bengali performing arts based in Dhaka. It began with a vision to improve the lives of children from the slum areas of Dhaka through music education. In its first year, MFD was funded by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP).
mfd.story2: What makes MFD unique is its holistic approach. Music is the entry point — but the goal is to grow well-rounded, empowered individuals. Students learn to sing, dance, and perform, and they also receive academic tutoring, vocational skills, and a community that believes in their future.
mfd.story3: The children of MFD come from some of Dhaka's most under-resourced communities. Many arrive without access to formal education. Through MFD, they find their voice — literally and figuratively.
mfd.ctaSubtitle: Visit the official Music for Development website to learn more, read student stories, and find out how you can support this work.

rows.label: Our Story
rows.title: Our Story
rows.body: Music for Development (MFD) started in 2009 as a project of Shurer Dhara, a premier college for Rabindrasangeet and Bengali performing arts based in Dhaka. It began with a vision to improve the lives of the children from the slum areas of Dhaka through music education. In its first year, MFD was funded by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP).

rows.label: What We Do
rows.title: What We Do
rows.body: Our students are underprivileged children from different slum areas of Dhaka and its adjoining areas. Although music is our main focus we also give the children of MFD extra tutoring in mathematics, Bengali, English, coding and vocational training — helping these underprivileged children to grow up to be successful and happy citizens of Bangladesh.

rows.label: Showcase
rows.title: Show Case of Events by Our Students
rows.body: Our students love singing, dancing, acting, reciting. Enjoy some photos of our students' performances.

rows.label: Our Children
rows.title: Meet the MFD Children
rows.body: Read the inspiring stories of the MFD Children.

pillars.title: Music Education
pillars.desc: Students receive structured training in singing, dancing, acting, and reciting — arts woven into the fabric of Bengali culture.

pillars.title: Academic Support
pillars.desc: Beyond music, MFD provides extra tutoring in mathematics, Bengali, English, and coding to help children succeed in school.

pillars.title: Vocational Training
pillars.desc: Older students receive practical skills to build a sustainable livelihood — ensuring music is the start, not the ceiling, of their potential.

pillars.title: Community & Performance
pillars.desc: Regular showcases and public performances build confidence and give families a visible symbol of pride and progress.

timeline.event: MFD founded as a project of Shurer Dhara, Dhaka. Initial funding from the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP).
First public performance by MFD children at a national cultural event in Dhaka.
Expanded to additional slum communities in Dhaka's outskirts, reaching over 200 children.
Introduced coding and vocational training alongside music to broaden children's futures.
Adapted programming during the pandemic — delivering mentorship and support to keep students connected.
Over 1,000 children have passed through the MFD programme since its inception.

footer.tagline: Founded by Prof. (Dr.) Rezwana Choudhury Bannya. Keeping the flow of Tagore's melody alive.
